data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE eight-child roster (S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the materialization-primitive slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role):
- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half: its AC1 (materialize(ref) produces a directory byte-matching the committed tree at the resolved SHA INCLUDING export-ignore-marked files, faithful not git-archive), AC2 (LFS pointer detection + submodule handling defined/documented), AC3 (atomic population; reader never observes a partial tree; crashed materialization leaves only tmp/ cleaned on startup), AC6 (concurrent materialize() of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock). S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3) owns the complementary half — re-rooting the gate file tools onto this child's snapshot dir so the gate "never the server's working tree". Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2 manifest-step binding + reproducible re-run). This child PROVIDES the immutable-SHA basis (its coalesced fetch -> resolve ref to immutable SHA) that makes reproducibility achievable, but does not sign (its Scope: "Out: ... signing (S4)"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S4 covers signing).
- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered by S3 (fa49 AC4 reads in-place) + S4 (30b3 AC3 bars signing) + S6 docs. This child's materialize() takes a source_mode param and its AC5 notes "attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs" — it RESPECTS the mode but the in-place-read behavior and signing bar are owned by S3/S4. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark — S2 — without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child OWNS the git-lock-contention + atomic-population + no-worktree half (its AC3 atomic all-or-nothing population, crashed-materialization residue cleanup; its AC6 concurrent materialize() of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock — via per-materialization GIT_INDEX_FILE temp index; the coalesced fetch is the only lock-taking step). S2 (70c7) owns single-flight + delete-on-last-close; S2b (b73c) owns the janitor watermark reclamation. No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the PRIMARY OWNER of the RAISE half: its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed). S6 (1ba5 AC1/AC2) owns the documentation half (the requirement + message + remedy in a named file). The fetch is this child's coalesced lock-taking step, so this child is the natural raise site. Clean impl/docs split.
- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the PRIMARY OWNER of the IMPLEMENTATION: its AC4 (no external tar binary invoked; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux), backed by its What (extraction never shells to tar; temp root from tempfile.gettempdir()/mkdtemp 0700 overridable via REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, created OUTSIDE the repo/.git; fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback on a local FS). S6 (1ba5 AC5) documents the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR knob + EFS/NFS flock caveat. Heart of this child's portability obligation. Clean.
- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered by S2b (b73c AC9 watermark/grace/max-age/reverify configurable with documented defaults) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it consumes the default ref (default origin/main in its materialize() signature) but does not own the tunable-config surface. Not an absence (S2b/S3 own it).
- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — covered by S2/S2b (b73c AC10 ADR records cache/janitor architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1 manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision recorded in the ADR) + S4b (e308 ADR 0002). The parent's ADR note lists "read-tree+checkout-index over git archive" as one of the captured novel patterns — that pattern ORIGINATES in this child's What (read-tree into temp index then checkout-index), but the ADR AUTHORING is assigned to S2/S4 per the parent ("authored in S2/S4"); this child supplies the decision content, the authoring home is S2. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (it has no ADR-authoring AC and its Scope does not claim one). Not an absence (S2/S4/S4b author the ADR).
- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this child is S1, the de-risking-first primitive) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA" + "never the server's mutable checkout" + "faithful materialization" core is precisely this child's deliverable: the resolve-to-immutable-SHA + faithful read-tree/checkout-index extraction + atomic all-or-nothing population is the primitive every downstream guarantee (S2 caches it, S3 re-roots gates onto it, S4 signs over its SHA) is built on. The "client-specified" surfacing is S5; the "signed/verified_at_sha" act is S4; the "local preserves verify-before-push" reader/enforcer halves are S3/S4. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Scope explicitly delegates the cache/janitor to S2 (and S2b), gate wiring to S3, signing to S4, surfaces to S5 — each present in the roster and claiming its slice. Its What defers the credential-message detail to S6 ("see S6"); S6 AC1/AC2 claim it. Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4/S4b seam (the ref-coherence story S4b e308 now owns) — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the resolved-SHA basis this child produces into the attestation hashing path; it is downstream of this child's resolve-to-immutable-SHA output and is not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence) remain CLAIMED by children (S3/S4/S4b), so the union covers them. G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the materialization-primitive slice (resolve-to-immutable-SHA, faithful read-tree+checkout-index extraction including export-ignore files, LFS/submodule handling, atomic all-or-nothing population + crash-residue cleanup, no-tar/no-hardcoded-/tmp/flock+mkdir-fallback portability, descriptive credential-error raise + attested-fail-closed, no index.lock/config.lock contention across SHAs) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half of parent AC1, the git-lock/atomic-population half of AC4, the raise half of AC5, and the implementation of AC6. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
